zh.lifeSummary: 當Cicely de Warren於1424年5月3日出生在英國爾波易頓時，她的父親Lawrence de Warrene30歲，母親Lady Margery de Bulkeley36歲。她於1435年1月22日與John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire在英國英格蘭爾波易頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1478年10月在她的家鄉享年54歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Cicely de Warren于1424年5月3日出生在英国爾波易頓时，她的父亲Lawrence de Warrene30岁，母亲Lady Margery de Bulkeley36岁。她于1435年1月22日与John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire在英国英格兰爾波易頓结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1478年10月在她的家乡享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: Warren
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Warin, Werin, a borrowing of ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. Compare Waring .

English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from La Varrenne in Seine-Maritime, France, named with a Gaulish element probably descriptive of alluvial land or sandy soil. This was the name of a major Norman family after the Conquest. In Ireland, this name has been Gaelicized as Bharain.
Irish: adopted as an English form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane , Warner ).

variants.name: Warrens
meanings: English: variant of Warren with genitival or excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Warrener
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name from Old French warrennier ‘warrener’, an officer employed to watch over game in a park or game preserve. Compare Warren .

variants.name: Murnane
meanings: Irish (Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin, a shortened and dissimilated form of Ó Manannáin ‘descendant of Manannán’, name borne in Celtic mythology by a sea god associated with the Isle of Man. All three syllables appear in the variant Marrinan. This name has also been Anglicized as Warren and Warner .

variants.name: Warne
meanings: occasionally a habitational name from Wearne in Somerset. The place takes its name from a lost stream-name based on British verno- ‘alder, swamp’.

variants.name: Waring
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of Warren , from the Norman personal name Warin, derived from ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. The name was popular in France and among the Normans (see Guerin ).

nameMeanings.name: Cicely
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Cecily .  This was a common form of the name in the Middle Ages, and in the 20th century was well known as the name of the British actress and singer  Cicely Courtneidge  (  1893–1980 ).

variants.name: Cecily
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Cicely de Warren was born on 3 May 1424, in Poynton, Cheshire, England, her father, Lawrence de Warrene, was 30 and her mother, Lady Margery de Bulkeley, was 36. She married John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire on 22 January 1435, in Poynton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died in October 1478, in her hometown, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Cicely de Warren est née le 3 mai 1424, à Poynton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Lawrence de Warrene, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lady Margery de Bulkeley, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire le 22 janvier 1435, à Poynton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en octobre 1478, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Cicely de Warren am 3. Mai 1424 in Poynton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Lawrence de Warrene 30 und ihre Mutter Lady Margery de Bulkeley 36. Sie hat am 22. Januar 1435 in Poynton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb im Oktober 1478 im Alter von 54 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Cicely de Warren è nata il il 3 maggio 1424 nel Poynton, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Lawrence de Warrene, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Lady Margery de Bulkeley, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire il 22 gennaio 1435, nel Poynton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel ottobre 1478, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Cicely de Warrenが1424年5月3日にイギリスチェシュアイアポイントンで生まれた時，父Lawrence de Warreneは30歳，母Lady Margery de Bulkeleyは36歳であった。1435年1月22日にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアポイントンでJohn Davenport of Bramhall, Esquireと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1478年10月に故郷で54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Cicely de Warren(이)가 1424년 5월 3일 영국 체실르 포이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lawrence de Warrene(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margery de Bulkeley(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1435년 1월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 포이턴에서 John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1478년 10월 그녀의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Cicely de Warren nasceu em 3 de maio de 1424, em Poynton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Lawrence de Warrene, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margery de Bulkeley, tinha 36 anos. Ela casou-se com John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire em 22 de janeiro de 1435, em Poynton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em outubro de 1478, em sua cidade natal, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Cicely de Warren родилась 3 мая 1424 г. в местности поинтон, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Lawrence de Warrene было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margery de Bulkeley, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire 22 января 1435 г. в местности поинтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла октябрь 1478 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Cicely de Warren nació el 3 de mayo de 1424, en Poynton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Lawrence de Warrene, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Lady Margery de Bulkeley, tenía 36 años. Se casó con John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire el 22 de enero de 1435, en Poynton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en octubre de 1478, en su pueblo, a la edad de 54 años.
